title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to Computational Fluid Dynamics, Governing Equations of
fluid flow and heat transfer, Conservative form of Navier-Stokes equation,
General transport equation. Physical and mathematical classifications of
partial differential equations. Elliptic, parabolic and hyperbolic equations,
Comparison of experimental, theoretical and numerical approaches;
applications of CFD.

title: Module 2
content:
Discretization-converting derivatives to their finite difference forms-
Taylor’s series approach and polynomial fitting approach.  Central
difference, backward difference, and forward difference of first and second
order derivatives. Solution of partial differential equations using finite
difference equations.  Discretization error, truncation error, round off
error. Consistency and numerical stability. Iterative convergence,
condition for convergence, rate of convergence.  Termination of iteration.
Boundary and Initial conditions

title: Module 3
content:
Introduction to finite volume method. Finite volume method for steady
one–dimensional conduction problems.  one–dimensional transient heat
conduction problems -explicit, implicit, Crank- Nicholson schemes, under
and over relaxations, handling of boundary conditions; dealing with
Dirichlet, Neumann, and Robins type boundary conditions; two-
dimensional steady state conduction problems; point-by-point and line-by-
line method of solution; tri-diagonal matrix algorithm.

title: Module 4
content:
Finite volume method for steady-state diffusion and convection-diffusion
problems; Central difference and Upwind schemes for convection and
diffusion problems.  Two dimensional incompressible viscous flows.
Staggered grid. Pressure correction methods. Solution algorithm for
pressure-velocity coupling in steady flows- SIMPLE algorithm to solve
Navier - Stokes equations.  Computer graphics techniques to present CFD
results.
